entries.title: ACₙ is 3-ω̄-critical
entries.decoded: For m ≥ 3 (so the circulant tournament ACₙ has n = 2m+1 ≥ 7 vertices), ACₙ is 3-ω̄-critical: its directed clique number ω̄(ACₙ) = 3, and deleting any single vertex leaves ω̄ = 2. This is the building block giving the infinite k = 3 family for Conjecture 5.10.

formal.name: AC_kcritical3
formal.verbatim: Theorem AC_kcritical3 : (3 <= m)%N -> kcritical 3 (AC m' : tournament).

entries.title: ACₙ is vertex-transitive

informal.text: ACₙ is vertex-transitive (as any Cayley digraph: translations act transitively) — the M2 exit fact feeding the M3 criticality reduction.

entries.decoded: The circulant tournament ACₙ is vertex-transitive: its automorphism group acts transitively on the vertices (for any two vertices there is a digraph automorphism — here a translation, as for any Cayley digraph — sending one to the other). This is the symmetry fact that reduces the criticality check to a single deleted vertex.

formal.name: AC_vertex_transitive
formal.verbatim: Lemma AC_vertex_transitive : vertex_transitiveb AC.

entries.title: CL1 — bilateral controlled-lifting for Strong Arc Decompositions

informal.text: Conjecture There exists an ineteger $ k $ so that every $ k $ -arc-connected digraph contains a pair of arc-disjoint strongly connected spanning subdigraphs?

source.name: OPG
source.attribution: library-internal lifting lemma toward Bang-Jensen–Yeo SAD

entries.decoded: For every digraph D and every vertex set V1 (with V2 its complement) such that both sides have at least 2 vertices, if each induced subdigraph D[V1] and D[V2] admits a Strong Arc Decomposition, and each of the two bridge out-cuts — the arcs leaving V1 (outcut V1) and the arcs leaving V2 (outcut V2) — can be split into two disjoint, nonempty, covering arc parts, then D itself admits a Strong Arc Decomposition. A relative (SAD-from-SAD) lifting target, provable without resolving WC3 or the general Bang-Jensen–Yeo conjecture.

formal.name: CL1_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition CL1_statement : Prop :=
  forall (D : diGraphType) (V1 : {set D}),
    let V2 := ~: V1 in
    (2 <= #|V1|)%N -> (2 <= #|V2|)%N ->
    SAD (induced_digraph V1) -> SAD (induced_digraph V2) ->
    (exists B1 B2 : {set (D * D)},
       [/\ [disjoint B1 & B2], B1 :|: B2 = outcut V1, B1 != set0 & B2 != set0]) ->
    (exists C1 C2 : {set (D * D)},
       [/\ [disjoint C1 & C2], C1 :|: C2 = outcut V2, C1 != set0 & C2 != set0]) ->
    SAD D.

grounding.name: CL1_premises_inhabited
grounding.gloss: the six premises are simultaneously satisfiable — witnessed by the 6-vertex host H6 (two bidirected triangles + a two-way bridge); axiom-free.

entries.faithfulness: Each side needs a SAD, and the smallest SAD-admitting digraph is the bidirected triangle (no 2-vertex digraph has a SAD), so the non-vacuity witness uses 3-vertex sides, not 2-vertex ones.

entries.title: Linear-forest ordering = 3/4-cycle transversal (the equivalence target)

informal.text: The equivalence target: [T] has a linear-forest ordering iff there is an arc set whose underlying simple graph is a linear forest and which hits every directed 3-cycle and every directed 4-cycle. (For tournaments the back-arc graph of a degree-2 order being acyclic is governed by short cycles; this is the open characterization in path_matching_fas/docs/q2_acyclicity_core.md.)

entries.decoded: For every tournament T, T has a linear-forest ordering if and only if there exists an arc set F whose underlying simple graph is a linear forest (acyclic, every vertex of degree at most 2) AND which hits every directed 3-cycle and every directed 4-cycle (every directed triangle and every directed 4-cycle uses some arc of F). Note F is only required to meet 3- and 4-cycles here — a strictly weaker hitting requirement than a full feedback arc set. Stated as a Prop target; the characterization is open.

formal.name: LFO_iff_34transversal_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition LFO_iff_34transversal_statement : Prop :=
  forall T : tournament,
    has_LFO T <->
    (exists F : {set T * T}, linear_forest (farc_graph F) /\ hits_34cycles F).

entries.title: T4 = ACₙ[C₃] is 4-ω̄-critical

informal.text: T4 is 4-ω̄-critical

entries.decoded: The tournament T4 (the lexicographic product ACₙ[C₃], for n = 2m+1 with m ≥ 3) is 4-ω̄-critical: kcritical 4 T4 unfolds to ω̄(T4) = 4 and, for EVERY vertex v, ω̄(T4 − v) = 4.-1 = 3. This is the single-witness criticality fact (the building block); the arbitrarily-large family statement of Conjecture 5.10 is conjecture_5_10_at_k4, not this lemma.

formal.name: T4_kcritical4
formal.verbatim: Theorem T4_kcritical4 : kcritical 4 T4.

entries.title: AACL Conj 5.10 — the host T₅ is 5-ω̄-critical

informal.text: For every integer $k\geq 3$, there is an infinite number of $k$-$\operatorname{\overrightarrow{\omega}}$-critical tournaments.

source.name: arXiv 2310.04265
source.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (Clique number of tournaments, 2023)

entries.decoded: The tournament T₅ = ACₙ[ACₙ] (the lexicographic product of the circulant ACₙ with itself, for odd n = 2m+1 with m ≥ 3) is 5-ω̄-critical: its directed clique number ω̄(T₅) equals 5, and deleting any single vertex v drops it to ω̄(T₅ − v) = 4.

formal.name: T5_kcritical5
formal.verbatim: Theorem T5_kcritical5 : kcritical 5 T5.

entries.title: Bang-Jensen–Yeo SAD at K = 3 (working conjecture WC3)

informal.text: Conjecture There exists an ineteger $ k $ so that every $ k $ -arc-connected digraph contains a pair of arc-disjoint strongly connected spanning subdigraphs?

entries.decoded: Every nonempty digraph D (0 < #|D|) that is 3-arc-strong admits a Strong Arc Decomposition, i.e. its arc set partitions into two disjoint arc classes that each span all vertices and are each strongly connected. This is the K = 3 instance of the Bang-Jensen–Yeo SAD conjecture: if true it settles that conjecture with K = 3, and a 3-arc-strong counterexample would refute it.

formal.name: WC3_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition WC3_statement : Prop :=
  forall D : diGraphType, (0 < #|D|)%N -> arc_strong D 3 -> SAD D.

entries.title: Minimum acyclic number of oriented triangle-free graphs — combinatorial core (a⃗)

informal.text: There exists a least integer $\mathrm{mader}_{\delta^0}(TT_k)$ such that every digraph $D$ with $\delta^0(D) \geq \mathrm{mader}_{\delta^0}(TT_k)$ contains a subdivision of $TT_k$.

source.name: arXiv 2403.02298
source.attribution: Aboulker, Bousquet, de Verclos et al.

entries.decoded: There is a sequence g : nat -> nat such that, for every order n > 0: (lower bound) every digon-free digraph D on exactly n vertices whose underlying graph is triangle-free has an acyclic induced subdigraph of size at least g(n); and (attainment) some such order-n graph has no acyclic induced set of size g(n)+1, which pins g(n) to the exact minimum acyclic number a⃗(n). This is the combinatorial core of the Θ(√(n·log n)) conjecture, with the asymptotic envelope abstracted into the existence of such a binding sequence g.

formal.name: avec_core_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition avec_core_statement : Prop :=
  exists g : nat -> nat,
    forall n : nat, (0 < n)%N ->
      (* lower bound: every order-n oriented triangle-free D has an acyclic set of size g n *)
      (forall D : diGraphType,
         #|D| = n -> oriented_dg D -> underlying_triangle_free D ->
         acyclic_number_ge D (g n)) /\
      (* attained — pins g to the MINIMUM a⃗(n): some order-n such D has no acyclic set of
         size g n + 1 (without this, g := 1 satisfies the lower bound vacuously).

entries.title: Bang-Jensen–Yeo Strong Arc Decomposition Conjecture

informal.text: Conjecture There exists an ineteger $ k $ so that every $ k $ -arc-connected digraph contains a pair of arc-disjoint strongly connected spanning subdigraphs?

entries.decoded: There is an absolute constant K such that every nonempty digraph D (0 < #|D|) that is K-arc-strong admits a Strong Arc Decomposition — a partition of its arc set into two disjoint arc classes, each spanning all vertices and each strongly connected (equivalently, a pair of arc-disjoint strongly connected spanning subdigraphs). The constant K is existentially bound, so the statement asserts that some finite arc-strength threshold forces such a decomposition. (Known: K = 2 fails — infinite obstruction families exist; no 3-arc-strong obstruction is known.)

formal.name: bang_jensen_yeo_SAD_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition bang_jensen_yeo_SAD_statement : Prop :=
  exists K : nat,
    forall D : diGraphType, (0 < #|D|)%N -> arc_strong D K -> SAD D.

entries.title: Bermond–Thomassen Conjecture

informal.text: Conjecture For every positive integer $ k $ , every digraph with minimum out-degree at least $ 2k-1 $ contains $ k $ disjoint cycles.

entries.decoded: For every nonempty digraph D (0 < #|D|) and every k, if every vertex has out-degree at least 2k−1 (pointwise, forall v: 2*k−1 ≤ outdeg v), then D contains a packing of k directed cycles that are pairwise vertex-disjoint: there is a list P of seqs, each a directed cycle (cycle_pack P), with all members pairwise sharing no vertex (vtx_disjoint_pack P) and size P = k.

formal.name: bermond_thomassen_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition bermond_thomassen_statement : Prop :=
  forall (D : diGraphType) (k : nat),
    (0 < #|D|)%N ->
    (forall v : D, (2 * k - 1 <= outdeg v)%N) ->
    exists P : seq (seq D),
      [/\ cycle_pack P, vtx_disjoint_pack P & size P = k].

entries.title: Caccetta–Häggkvist Conjecture

informal.text: Conjecture Every simple digraph of order $ n $ with minimum outdegree at least $ r $ has a cycle with length at most $ \lceil n/r\rceil $

entries.decoded: For every loopless digraph D on n vertices (0 < #|D|) in which every vertex has out-degree at least r, with r ≥ 1, there is a directed cycle of length at most ⌈n/r⌉ (written as (n+r−1) ÷ r). The nonemptiness guard 0 < #|D|, the looplessness condition (no vertex v has an arc v --> v), and r ≥ 1 are all stated explicitly.

formal.name: caccetta_haggkvist_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition caccetta_haggkvist_statement : Prop :=
  forall (D : diGraphType) (r : nat),
    (0 < #|D|)%N -> (forall v : D, ~~ (v --> v)) -> (0 < r)%N ->
    (forall v : D, (r <= outdeg v)%N) ->
    exists c : seq D, dicycle c /\ (size c <= (#|D| + r - 1) %/ r)%N.

entries.title: Caccetta–Häggkvist Conjecture — triangle case (r = n/3)

informal.text: Conjecture Every simple digraph of order $ n $ with minimum outdegree at least $ r $ has a cycle with length at most $ \lceil n/r\rceil $

entries.decoded: The famous triangle special case, over oriented (digon-free) digraphs: for every nonempty oriented digraph D (0 < #|D|) in which every vertex has out-degree at least n/3 — encoded as n ≤ 3·outdeg(v) for all v — there is a directed cycle of length exactly 3 (a directed triangle).

formal.name: caccetta_haggkvist_triangle_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition caccetta_haggkvist_triangle_statement : Prop :=
  forall D : orientedDigraph,
    (0 < #|D|)%N -> (forall v : D, (#|D| <= 3 * outdeg v)%N) ->
    exists c : seq D, dicycle c /\ size c = 3.

entries.title: ACₙ has n vertices
entries.decoded: The circulant tournament ACₙ has exactly n vertices (n = 2m+1, the order of the underlying cyclic group ℤ/nℤ).

formal.name: card_AC
formal.verbatim: Lemma card_AC : #|AC| = n.

entries.title: Order of T4 = ACₙ[C₃] is 3n

informal.text: T4 has order 3n

entries.decoded: The tournament T4 = ACₙ[C₃] (with n = 2m+1, m ≥ 3) has exactly 3n vertices (#|T4| = n·3), since ACₙ has n vertices and C₃ has 3 and the lexicographic product multiplies orders.

formal.name: card_T4
formal.verbatim: Lemma card_T4 : #|T4| = (n * 3)%N.

entries.title: AACL Conj 5.10 — order of the host T₅ is n²

informal.text: For every integer $k\geq 3$, there is an infinite number of $k$-$\operatorname{\overrightarrow{\omega}}$-critical tournaments.

source.name: arXiv 2310.04265
source.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (Clique number of tournaments, 2023)

entries.decoded: The host tournament T₅ = ACₙ[ACₙ] has exactly n² vertices (n = 2m+1), since the lexicographic product of two ACₙ's, each of order n, has n·n vertices. This is what makes distinct n yield arbitrarily large critical tournaments.

formal.name: card_T5
formal.verbatim: Lemma card_T5 : #|T5| = (n * n)%N.

entries.title: Cheng–Keevash Conjecture 1 — directed path of length 2·δ⁺

informal.text: Conjecture Every oriented graph with minimum outdegree $ k $ contains a directed path of length $ 2k $ .

source.name: OPG
source.attribution: Thomassé (also attributed in the Caccetta-Häggkvist circle)

entries.decoded: For every oriented digraph D (loopless, digon-free) with 0 < #|D| and every d such that every vertex has out-degree at least d (d ≤ outdeg v for all v), the longest directed path of D has length at least 2d (2·d ≤ ell D, where ell D is the number of arcs on a longest dipath). Equivalently: minimum out-degree ≥ d forces a directed path with 2d arcs.

formal.name: cheng_keevash_conj1_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition cheng_keevash_conj1_statement : Prop :=
  forall (D : orientedDigraph) (d : nat),
    0 < #|D| -> (forall v : D, d <= outdeg v) -> 2 * d <= ell D.

entries.title: Chordal directed graphs are not χ⃗-bounded

informal.text: Chordal directed graphs are NOT directed-χ-bounded (arXiv:2202.01006): the class C₃ has unbounded dichromatic number, i.e. [dichromatic_bounded] FAILS on C₃.

source.name: arXiv 2202.01006
source.attribution: Aboulker, Bousquet, de Verclos et al. (2022)

entries.decoded: It is NOT the case that the chordal class C₃ is dichromatic-bounded: there is no single constant B such that every digraph in C₃ is B-dicolourable (χ⃗ ≤ B). Here C₃ is the class of digon-free digraphs with no induced transitive triangle TT₃ and no induced (chordless) directed cycle of length ≥ 4, so the dichromatic number is unbounded over this class.

formal.name: chordal_not_dichromatic_bounded_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition chordal_not_dichromatic_bounded_statement : Prop :=
  ~ dichromatic_bounded chordal_C3.

entries.title: Cheng–Keevash Conj 1 (Thomassé directed-path) — proved at δ = 2

informal.text: Conjecture Every oriented graph with minimum outdegree $ k $ contains a directed path of length $ 2k $ .

source.name: OPG
source.attribution: Thomassé (also attributed in the Caccetta-Häggkvist circle)

entries.decoded: For every nonempty oriented graph D (0 < #|D|, no loops, no digons) in which every vertex has out-degree at least 2, the longest directed simple path has length at least 4 (ℓ(D) ≥ 4). This is the δ = 2 case of the conjecture that minimum out-degree δ forces a directed path of length 2δ, proved unconditionally as a corollary of the general Lemma 7 / Theorem 4 bound.

formal.name: ck_conj1_delta2
formal.verbatim:
Corollary ck_conj1_delta2 (D : orientedDigraph) :
  0 < #|D| -> (forall v : D, 2 <= outdeg v) -> 4 <= ell D.

entries.title: Cheng–Keevash Conj 1 (Thomassé directed-path) — proved at δ = 3

informal.text: Conjecture Every oriented graph with minimum outdegree $ k $ contains a directed path of length $ 2k $ .

source.name: OPG
source.attribution: Thomassé (also attributed in the Caccetta-Häggkvist circle)

entries.decoded: For every nonempty oriented graph D (0 < #|D|, no loops, no digons) in which every vertex has out-degree at least 3, the longest directed simple path has length at least 6 (ℓ(D) ≥ 6); ℓ(D) counts arcs. This is the δ = 3 case of the conjecture that minimum out-degree δ forces a directed path of length 2δ, proved unconditionally.

formal.name: ck_conj1_delta3
formal.verbatim:
Theorem ck_conj1_delta3 (D : orientedDigraph) :
  0 < #|D| -> (forall v : D, 3 <= outdeg v) -> 6 <= ell D.

entries.title: Cheng–Keevash Conj 1 at δ = 3 — explicit 6-arc path

informal.text: Conjecture Every oriented graph with minimum outdegree $ k $ contains a directed path of length $ 2k $ .

source.name: OPG
source.attribution: Thomassé (also attributed in the Caccetta-Häggkvist circle)

entries.decoded: For every nonempty oriented graph D (0 < #|D|, no loops, no digons) in which every vertex has out-degree at least 3, there exist a start vertex x and a sequence s of 6 vertices forming a directed simple path from x (dipath x s with size s = 6); i.e. a directed simple path with 6 arcs exists. This is the path-existence (unfolded) form of the δ = 3 case of the directed-path conjecture.

formal.name: ck_conj1_delta3_path
formal.verbatim:
Corollary ck_conj1_delta3_path (D : orientedDigraph) :
  0 < #|D| -> (forall v : D, 3 <= outdeg v) ->
  exists x : D, exists s : seq D, dipath x s /\ size s = 6.

entries.title: Cheng–Keevash Theorem 4 (oriented) — ℓ(D) ≥ 2δ − ⌊(δ−1)/2⌋

informal.text: Conjecture Every oriented graph with minimum outdegree $ k $ contains a directed path of length $ 2k $ .

source.name: OPG
source.attribution: Thomassé (also attributed in the Caccetta-Häggkvist circle)

entries.decoded: For every nonempty oriented graph D (0 < #|D|, no loops, no digons) and every δ such that every vertex has out-degree at least δ, the longest directed simple path satisfies ℓ(D) ≥ 2δ − ⌊(δ−1)/2⌋ (nat truncated subtraction; the ⌊·⌋/2 coded as (δ−1)./2). This is the general unconditional lower bound; at δ = 2 it gives the 2δ = 4 conjecture bound exactly (2·2 − ⌊1/2⌋ = 4), and it is the partial-progress envelope toward the full 2δ directed-path conjecture.

formal.name: ck_theorem4_oriented
formal.verbatim:
Theorem ck_theorem4_oriented (D : orientedDigraph) (delta : nat) :
  0 < #|D| -> (forall v : D, delta <= outdeg v) ->
  2 * delta - (delta - 1)./2 <= ell D.

entries.title: Aboulker–Charbit–Naserasr Conjecture 2 — Forb(H) is χ-bounded iff underlying H is a forest

informal.text: Conjecture 2 (1605.07411): Forb(H) is χ-bounded iff underlying H is a forest For an oriented graph [H], the class of oriented graphs with no induced copy of [H] is χ-bounded iff the underlying graph of [H] is a forest.

entries.decoded: For every digon-free (oriented) graph H: the class of oriented graphs that contain no induced copy of H is χ-bounded if and only if the underlying undirected graph of H is a forest. Here χ-bounded means a single binding function f bounds the ordinary undirected chromatic number χ(underlying G) ≤ f(ω(underlying G)) for every nonempty member G of the class.

formal.name: conj2_1605_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition conj2_1605_statement : Prop :=
  forall H : diGraphType,
    oriented_dg H ->
    ( chi_bounded_under (fun G => oriented_dg G /\ ind_free H G)
      <-> oriented_forest H ).

entries.title: Conjecture 3 (Aboulker–Bousquet et al.) — a⃗(n) = Θ(√(n·ln n))

informal.text: There exists a least integer $\mathrm{mader}_{\delta^0}(TT_k)$ such that every digraph $D$ with $\delta^0(D) \geq \mathrm{mader}_{\delta^0}(TT_k)$ contains a subdivision of $TT_k$.

source.name: arXiv 2403.02298
source.attribution: Aboulker, Bousquet, de Verclos et al.

entries.decoded: For every nat-sequence av that genuinely equals the extremal minimum acyclic number a⃗ (is_avec av: for each order n > 0, av n is both a lower bound on the acyclic number of every n-vertex oriented triangle-free digraph and attained by some such digraph), the real sequence n ↦ av n is Θ of env_a(n) = √(n·ln n). Θ is the two-sided Landau predicate: there exist positive reals c₁, c₂ and a threshold N with c₁·√(n·ln n) ≤ av n ≤ c₂·√(n·ln n) for all n ≥ N.

formal.name: conj3_avec_Theta_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition conj3_avec_Theta_statement : Prop :=
  forall av : nat -> nat,
    is_avec av ->
    is_Theta (fun n => INR (av n)) env_a.

entries.title: Conjecture 3 (Aboulker–Bousquet et al.) — existential form: a⃗ exists and is Θ(√(n·ln n))

informal.text: There exists a least integer $\mathrm{mader}_{\delta^0}(TT_k)$ such that every digraph $D$ with $\delta^0(D) \geq \mathrm{mader}_{\delta^0}(TT_k)$ contains a subdivision of $TT_k$.

source.name: arXiv 2403.02298
source.attribution: Aboulker, Bousquet, de Verclos et al.

entries.decoded: There exists a nat-sequence av that genuinely equals the extremal minimum acyclic number a⃗ (is_avec av) AND is Θ of env_a(n) = √(n·ln n) — i.e. there are positive reals c₁, c₂ and a threshold N with c₁·√(n·ln n) ≤ av n ≤ c₂·√(n·ln n) eventually. A concrete-witness reading equivalent in content to the universally-quantified form.

formal.name: conj3_avec_exists_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition conj3_avec_exists_statement : Prop :=
  exists av : nat -> nat,
    is_avec av /\ is_Theta (fun n => INR (av n)) env_a.

entries.title: Aboulker–Charbit–Naserasr Conjecture 4 — every oriented star is χ-bounding

informal.text: Conjecture 4: every oriented star is χ-bounding. (A special case of Conjecture 2, since a star is an oriented forest; stated separately as in the paper.)

entries.decoded: For every oriented star S (an orientation of K_{1,t}: one centre adjacent in the underlying graph to all others, with the leaves pairwise non-adjacent), the class of oriented graphs with no induced copy of S is χ-bounded — a single binding function bounds χ(underlying G) ≤ f(ω(underlying G)) for every nonempty member. A special case of Conjecture 2, since an oriented star is an oriented forest.

formal.name: conj4_1605_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition conj4_1605_statement : Prop :=
  forall S : diGraphType,
    oriented_star S ->
    chi_bounded_under (fun G => oriented_dg G /\ ind_free S G).

entries.title: Conjecture 4 (Aboulker–Bousquet et al.) — t⃗(n) = Θ(√(n/ln n))

informal.text: For any oriented star $S$, $\mathrm{Forb}(S)$ is $\chi$-bounded.

source.name: arXiv 2403.02298
source.attribution: Aboulker, Bousquet, de Verclos et al.

entries.decoded: For every nat-sequence tv that genuinely equals the extremal maximum dichromatic number t⃗ (is_tvec tv: for each order n > 0, every n-vertex oriented triangle-free digraph is tv n-dicolourable, χ⃗ ≤ tv n, and this is attained by some such digraph), the real sequence n ↦ tv n is Θ of env_t(n) = √(n/ln n). Θ here means there exist positive reals c₁, c₂ and a threshold N with c₁·√(n/ln n) ≤ tv n ≤ c₂·√(n/ln n) for all n ≥ N.

formal.name: conj4_tvec_Theta_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition conj4_tvec_Theta_statement : Prop :=
  forall tv : nat -> nat,
    is_tvec tv ->
    is_Theta (fun n => INR (tv n)) env_t.

entries.title: Conjecture 4 (Aboulker–Bousquet et al.) — existential form: t⃗ exists and is Θ(√(n/ln n))

informal.text: For any oriented star $S$, $\mathrm{Forb}(S)$ is $\chi$-bounded.

source.name: arXiv 2403.02298
source.attribution: Aboulker, Bousquet, de Verclos et al.

entries.decoded: There exists a nat-sequence tv that genuinely equals the extremal maximum dichromatic number t⃗ (is_tvec tv) AND is Θ of env_t(n) = √(n/ln n) — i.e. there are positive reals c₁, c₂ and a threshold N with c₁·√(n/ln n) ≤ tv n ≤ c₂·√(n/ln n) eventually. A concrete-witness reading equivalent in content to the universally-quantified form.

formal.name: conj4_tvec_exists_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition conj4_tvec_exists_statement : Prop :=
  exists tv : nat -> nat,
    is_tvec tv /\ is_Theta (fun n => INR (tv n)) env_t.

entries.title: Aboulker–Charbit–Naserasr Conjecture 5 — non-exceptional families of P₄-orientations are χ-bounding

informal.text: Conjecture 5: any non-empty family [forb] of orientations of P₄ that is neither the singleton [{→P₄}] nor the singleton [{P⁺(1,1,1)}] is χ-bounding. We encode "[forb] is a non-empty subset of Or(P₄) that is neither exceptional singleton" by: [forb] holds only of P₄-orientations, is inhabited, and contains a member that is neither the directed nor the alternating P₄ (this rules out both exceptional singletons).

entries.decoded: For every family forb of orientations of the 4-vertex path P₄ that is nonempty (some member exists) and contains a member that is neither the directed path →P₄ nor the alternating orientation P⁺(1,1,1) — which rules out both exceptional singletons — the class of oriented graphs with no induced copy of any member of forb is χ-bounded (a single f bounds χ(underlying G) ≤ f(ω(underlying G)) for every nonempty member).

formal.name: conj5_1605_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition conj5_1605_statement : Prop :=
  forall forb : diGraphType -> Prop,
    (forall P : diGraphType, forb P -> P4_underlying P) ->
    (exists P : diGraphType, forb P) ->
    (exists P : diGraphType, forb P /\ ~ is_dirP4 P /\ ~ is_altP4 P) ->
    chi_bounded_under (fun G => oriented_dg G /\ Forb_ind forb G).

entries.title: AACL Conjecture 3.12 — bounded-twin-width tournaments are ω̄-bounded

informal.text: Let $k\geq 1$. The class of tournaments with twin-width at most $k$ is $\operatorname{\overrightarrow{\chi}}$-bounded.

source.name: arXiv 2310.04265
source.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (Clique number of tournaments)

entries.decoded: For every k there is a binding function f_k (the existential ∃f sits inside the ∀k, so f may depend on k) such that every nonempty tournament T (0 < #|T|) whose twin-width is at most k (tww_le T k) is f_k(ω̄(T))-dicolourable, i.e. χ⃗(T) ≤ f_k(ω̄(T)) — dicolorableb T m encodes exactly χ⃗(T) ≤ m. In other words, within each bounded-twin-width class the dichromatic number is bounded by a function of the tournament clique number ω̄.

formal.name: conj_3_12_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition conj_3_12_statement : Prop :=
  forall k : nat,
    exists f : nat -> nat,
      forall T : tournament,
        (0 < #|T|)%N -> tww_le T k -> dicolorableb T (f (omegabar T)).

entries.title: AACL Conjecture 3.13 — one ordering bounding both backedge clique number and ordered twin-width

informal.text: There exists a function $f$, such that for every tournament $T$, there exists an ordering $\prec^{*}$ of $V(T)$ such that:

source.name: arXiv 2310.04265
source.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (Clique number of tournaments)

entries.decoded: There is a single function f (one ∃f over all tournaments) such that every nonempty tournament T (0 < #|T|) admits an ordering p of its vertices that simultaneously bounds two quantities by f(ω̄(T)): the backedge clique number ω(T^p) (bclique p ≤ f(ω̄(T))) AND the ordered twin-width of that ordering (otww_le p (f(ω̄(T)))). Both bounds use the same value f(ω̄(T)).

formal.name: conj_3_13_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition conj_3_13_statement : Prop :=
  exists f : nat -> nat,
    forall T : tournament,
      (0 < #|T|)%N ->
      exists p : {perm T},
        (bclique p <= f (omegabar T))%N /\ otww_le p (f (omegabar T)).

entries.title: AACL Conjecture 3.16 — a BST-ordering bounding the backedge clique number

informal.text: There exists a function $f$ such that, for every tournament $T$, there exists a $BST$-ordering $\prec$ of $T$ such:

source.name: arXiv 2310.04265
source.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (Clique number of tournaments)

entries.decoded: There is a single function f (one ∃f over all tournaments) such that every nonempty tournament T (0 < #|T|) admits an ordering p that is a BST-ordering (bst_order p) whose backedge clique number ω(T^p) is at most f(ω̄(T)) (bclique p ≤ f(ω̄(T))).

formal.name: conj_3_16_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition conj_3_16_statement : Prop :=
  exists f : nat -> nat,
    forall T : tournament,
      (0 < #|T|)%N ->
      exists p : {perm T},
        bst_order p /\ (bclique p <= f (omegabar T))%N.

entries.title: AACL Conjecture 5.10 — proved at k ∈ {3,4,5}

informal.text: For every integer $k\geq 3$, there is an infinite number of $k$-$\operatorname{\overrightarrow{\omega}}$-critical tournaments.

entries.decoded: For every k with 3 ≤ k ≤ 5 and every bound N, there exists a tournament T that is k-ω̄-critical (its directed clique number ω̄(T) = k and deleting any single vertex drops ω̄ to k−1) and has more than N vertices. Since N is arbitrary, this gives arbitrarily large — hence infinitely many — k-ω̄-critical tournaments for each k in {3,4,5}, establishing Conjecture 5.10 in that range.

formal.name: conjecture_5_10_at_345
formal.verbatim:
Theorem conjecture_5_10_at_345 (k : nat) : (3 <= k <= 5)%N ->
  forall N : nat, exists T : tournament, kcritical k T /\ (N < #|T|)%N.

entries.title: AACL Conjecture 5.10 — proved at k = 3

informal.text: For every integer $k\geq 3$, there is an infinite number of $k$-$\operatorname{\overrightarrow{\omega}}$-critical tournaments.

entries.decoded: For every bound N there exists a 3-ω̄-critical tournament T (directed clique number ω̄(T) = 3, with ω̄ dropping to 2 after deleting any single vertex) on more than N vertices. As N is arbitrary this yields arbitrarily large — thus infinitely many — 3-ω̄-critical tournaments, i.e. the k = 3 case of Conjecture 5.10.

formal.name: conjecture_5_10_at_k3
formal.verbatim:
Theorem conjecture_5_10_at_k3 (N : nat) :
  exists T : tournament, kcritical 3 T /\ (N < #|T|)%N.

entries.title: AACL Conjecture 5.10 — proved at k = 4

informal.text: For every integer $k\geq 3$, there is an infinite number of $k$-$\operatorname{\overrightarrow{\omega}}$-critical tournaments.

entries.decoded: For every bound N there exists a tournament T that is 4-ω̄-critical and has more than N vertices; hence there are arbitrarily large (infinitely many) 4-ω̄-critical tournaments. This is the k = 4 instance of Conjecture 5.10 (the witnesses being the family ACₙ[C₃]).

formal.name: conjecture_5_10_at_k4
formal.verbatim:
Theorem conjecture_5_10_at_k4 (N : nat) :
  exists T : tournament, kcritical 4 T /\ (N < #|T|)%N.

entries.title: AACL Conj 5.10 — proved at k = 5

informal.text: For every integer $k\geq 3$, there is an infinite number of $k$-$\operatorname{\overrightarrow{\omega}}$-critical tournaments.

source.name: arXiv 2310.04265
source.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (Clique number of tournaments, 2023)

entries.decoded: For every N there exists a tournament T that is 5-ω̄-critical (ω̄(T) = 5 and ω̄(T − v) = 4 for every vertex v) and has more than N vertices. This is the k = 5 instance of Conjecture 5.10: there are arbitrarily large (hence infinitely many) 5-ω̄-critical tournaments.

formal.name: conjecture_5_10_at_k5
formal.verbatim:
Theorem conjecture_5_10_at_k5 (N : nat) :
  exists T : tournament, kcritical 5 T /\ (N < #|T|)%N.

entries.title: AACL Conjecture 5.10 — infinitely many k-ω̄-critical tournaments

informal.text: For every integer $k\geq 3$, there is an infinite number of $k$-$\operatorname{\overrightarrow{\omega}}$-critical tournaments.

entries.decoded: For every k ≥ 3 and every bound N, there is a tournament T that is k-ω̄-critical (kcritical k T) and has more than N vertices (N < #|T|). Because the size bound N is universally quantified, this encodes "arbitrarily large k-ω̄-critical tournaments exist", i.e. infinitely many up to isomorphism, for each fixed k ≥ 3.

formal.name: conjecture_5_10_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition conjecture_5_10_statement : Prop :=
  forall k : nat, (3 <= k)%N ->
    forall N : nat, exists T : tournament, kcritical k T /\ (N < #|T|)%N.

entries.title: AACL Conjecture 5.8 — bounded ω̄-cluster from large ω̄ (f ≠ id weakening)

informal.text: Conjecture 5.8: the f ≠ id weakening — two functions f, ℓ with ω̄(T) ≥ f(k) forcing a size-≤ ℓ(k) subtournament of ω̄ ≥ k.

entries.decoded: There exist two functions f, ℓ : ℕ → ℕ such that for every tournament T and every k, if ω̄(T) ≥ f(k) then T has a vertex subset S of size at most ℓ(k) whose induced subtournament satisfies ω̄(sub_tournament S) ≥ k. This is the relaxed form of Question 5.9 in which the threshold on ω̄ is f(k) rather than k itself.

formal.name: conjecture_5_8_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition conjecture_5_8_statement : Prop :=
  exists f ell : nat -> nat,
    forall (T : tournament) (k : nat), (f k <= ω̄(T))%N ->
      exists S : {set T}, (#|S| <= ell k)%N /\ (k <= ω̄(sub_tournament S))%N.

entries.title: AACL Conjecture 5.3 — large domination forces a bounded ω̄-cluster

informal.text: There exist two functions $f$ and $\ell$ such that, for every integer $k$, every tournament $T$ with $\operatorname{dom}(T)\geq f(k)$ contains a subtournament $X$ with $|X|\leq\ell(k)$ and $\operatorname{\overrightarrow{\omega}}(X)\geq k$.

entries.decoded: There exist two functions f, ℓ : ℕ → ℕ such that for every tournament T and every k, if the domination number dom(T) ≥ f(k), then T has a vertex subset S of size at most ℓ(k) whose induced subtournament satisfies ω̄(sub_tournament S) ≥ k. Same bounded-witness conclusion as Conjecture 5.8 but with a domination-number hypothesis in place of the ω̄ hypothesis.

formal.name: dom_omega_cluster_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition dom_omega_cluster_statement : Prop :=
  exists f ell : nat -> nat,
    forall (T : tournament) (k : nat), (f k <= domnum T)%N ->
      exists S : {set T}, (#|S| <= ell k)%N /\ (k <= ω̄(sub_tournament S))%N.

entries.title: AACL Property 3.2 — dom(T) ≤ ω̄(T)

informal.text: Paper Property 3.2 — the lower-bound engine for ω̄.

entries.decoded: For a tournament T, the domination number domnum (the least size of a set X such that every vertex outside X is beaten by some member of X) is at most ω̄(T), the tournament clique number. This is the lower-bound engine for ω̄ (Property 3.2 of Aboulker–Aubian–Charbit–Lopes).

formal.name: domnum_le_omegabar
formal.verbatim: Theorem domnum_le_omegabar : domnum <= ω̄(T).

entries.title: EC-log Eulerian SAD milestone (open conjecture) — explicit constant C = 6

informal.text: The concrete proven form ([C = 6]): instantiates the existential. This is an EDGE (Qed-closed): the [C = 6] form implies the [∃ C] milestone.

entries.decoded: For every digraph D on at least 3 vertices that is Eulerian (in-degree = out-degree at every vertex), if D is k-arc-strong and the real inequality k ≥ 6·log₂(#|D|) holds (log₂ x = ln x / ln 2), then D admits a Strong Arc Decomposition. The concrete C = 6, n₀ = 3 instance of the Bang-Jensen–Yeo Eulerian logarithmic threshold; it implies the ∃C form.

formal.name: ec_log_c6_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition ec_log_c6_statement : Prop :=
  forall (D : diGraphType) (k : nat),
    (3 <= #|D|)%N ->
    eulerian D ->
    arc_strong D k ->
    INR k >= 6 * log2 (INR #|D|) ->
    SAD D.

entries.title: EC-log Eulerian SAD milestone — there is an absolute constant C

informal.text: The EC-log lemma (Bang-Jensen–Yeo Eulerian logarithmic milestone): there is an absolute constant — here the proven [C = 6] with threshold order [n₀ = 3] — such that every Eulerian digraph of order [n] whose arc-strength [k] meets the REAL threshold [k ≥ 6·log₂ n] admits a Strong Arc Decomposition. Faithful encoding: arc-strength is the nat [arc_strong D k] of [sad.v] ([λ(D) ≥ k]); the comparison to the real threshold [6·log₂ n] is [INR k >= 6 * log2 (INR n)] in [%R]. We GUARD [3 <= n] (the proven [n₀]); the parameter [k] is the witnessing arc-strength. Stated existentially over the constant [c = 6] so the milestone is "∃ C, …", matching its statement.

entries.decoded: There is a positive real constant c such that for every digraph D on at least 3 vertices that is Eulerian (in-degree = out-degree at every vertex), if D is k-arc-strong and k ≥ c·log₂(#|D|) (the comparison is over the reals, log₂ x = ln x / ln 2), then D admits a Strong Arc Decomposition. A logarithmic-arc-strength threshold guaranteeing SAD in the Eulerian regime of Bang-Jensen–Yeo, guarded by 3 ≤ #|D|.

formal.name: ec_log_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition ec_log_statement : Prop :=
  exists c : R,
    0 < c /\
    forall (D : diGraphType) (k : nat),
      (3 <= #|D|)%N ->
      eulerian D ->
      arc_strong D k ->
      INR k >= c * log2 (INR #|D|) ->
      SAD D.

entries.title: Erdős–Pósa property for long directed cycles

informal.text: Conjecture Let $ \ell \geq 2 $ be an integer. For every integer $ n\geq 0 $ , there exists an integer $ t_n=t_n(\ell) $ such that for every digraph $ D $ , either $ D $ has a $ n $ pairwise-disjoint directed cycles of length at least $ \ell $ , or there exists a set $ T $ of at most $ t_n $ vertices such that $ D-T $ has no directed cycles of length at least $ \ell $ .

entries.decoded: For every length threshold ℓ ≥ 2 and every n there is a bound t (a number depending on ℓ and n but not on the digraph) such that for every digraph D, either D contains n pairwise vertex-disjoint directed cycles each of length at least ℓ (a vertex-disjoint cycle pack of size n with every member of length ≥ ℓ), or there is a set T of at most t vertices that meets every directed cycle of length ≥ ℓ (meets_long_dicycles ℓ T).

formal.name: erdos_posa_long_dicycles_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition erdos_posa_long_dicycles_statement : Prop :=
  forall ell : nat, (2 <= ell)%N ->
  forall n : nat, exists t : nat,
    forall D : diGraphType,
      (exists P : seq (seq D),
         [/\ cycle_pack P, vtx_disjoint_pack P, size P = n &
             all (fun c => ell <= size c)%N P])
      \/
      (exists T : {set D}, (#|T| <= t)%N /\ meets_long_dicycles ell T).

entries.title: Hoàng–Reed Conjecture

informal.text: Conjecture Every digraph in which each vertex has outdegree at least $ k $ contains $ k $ directed cycles $ C_1, \ldots, C_k $ such that $ C_j $ meets $ \cup_{i=1}^{j-1}C_i $ in at most one vertex, $ 2 \leq j \leq k $ .

entries.decoded: For every nonempty digraph D (0 < #|D|) and every k, if every vertex has out-degree at least k (pointwise, forall v: k ≤ outdeg v), then D contains a pack P of k directed cycles (cycle_pack P, size P = k) with the intersection-forest property: for each position j > 0, the set of vertices on cycle j that also lie on some earlier cycle i < j has size at most one — i.e. each cycle meets the union of all the previous ones in at most one vertex.

formal.name: hoang_reed_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition hoang_reed_statement : Prop :=
  forall (D : diGraphType) (k : nat),
    (0 < #|D|)%N ->
    (forall v : D, (k <= outdeg v)%N) ->
    exists P : seq (seq D),
      [/\ cycle_pack P,
          size P = k &
          (* intersection-forest: cycle #j meets the union of the earlier ones
             in at most one vertex, for every 1 <= j < size P *)
          forall j : 'I_(size P), (0 < j)%N ->
            (#|[set v : D | (v \in nth [::] P j) &&
                 [exists i : 'I_(size P), (i < j)%N && (v \in nth [::] P i)]]| <= 1)%N].

entries.title: Jackson's Conjecture — Hamilton cycle in small d-diregular oriented graphs

informal.text: An directed graph is $ k $ -diregular if every vertex has indegree and outdegree at least $ k $ . Conjecture For $ d >2 $ , every $ d $ -diregular oriented graph on at most $ 4d+1 $ vertices has a Hamilton cycle.

entries.decoded: For every nonempty oriented (digon-free) digraph D (0 < #|D|) and every d > 2 (i.e. d ≥ 3) such that D is d-diregular (diregular D d, i.e. every vertex has in-degree EXACTLY d and out-degree EXACTLY d — not merely ≥ d) and D has at most 4d+1 vertices, there is a directed cycle whose size equals #|D|, i.e. a Hamilton directed cycle through every vertex.

formal.name: jackson_hamilton_small_diregular_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition jackson_hamilton_small_diregular_statement : Prop :=
  forall (D : orientedDigraph) (d : nat),
    (0 < #|D|)%N -> 2 < d -> diregular D d -> (#|D| <= 4 * d + 1)%N ->
    exists c : seq D, dicycle c /\ size c = #|D|.

entries.title: Uniqueness of the 2-ω̄-critical tournament (C₃)

informal.text: The explicit isomorphism C3 ≅ T sending 0,1,2 to the triangle.

entries.decoded: Every 2-ω̄-critical tournament T (i.e. T satisfying kcritical 2 T: ω̄(T) = 2 and deleting any single vertex drops ω̄ to 1) is digraph-isomorphic to C₃, the directed triangle. Hence C₃ is the unique 2-ω̄-critical tournament.

formal.name: kcritical2_uniq
formal.verbatim: Theorem kcritical2_uniq : dgiso (C3 : diGraphType) T.

entries.title: Proper subtournaments of a k-ω̄-critical tournament have ω̄ ≤ k−1

informal.text: Proper subtournaments of critical tournaments In a k-ω̄-critical tournament every *proper* subtournament has ω̄ ≤ k − 1: it omits some vertex, hence embeds into that vertex's deletion (the Question-5.9-failure mechanism, stated once; G1 of docs/k34_dossier.md).

entries.decoded: For every natural number k and every k-ω̄-critical tournament T, any vertex subset S that is NOT the whole vertex set (S ≠ [set: T], a proper subset) induces a subtournament with ω̄(sub_tournament S) ≤ k−1. A proper subset omits some vertex, so it embeds into that vertex's deletion, which has ω̄ = k−1 by criticality.

formal.name: kcritical_proper_sub
formal.verbatim:
Lemma kcritical_proper_sub (k : nat) (T : tournament) (S : {set T}) :
  kcritical k T -> S != [set: T] -> (ω̄(sub_tournament S) <= k.-1)%N.

entries.title: Lemma 7 (Cheng–Keevash) — dichotomy: long path or a deficient set

informal.text: Conjecture Every oriented graph with minimum outdegree $ k $ contains a directed path of length $ 2k $ .

source.name: OPG
source.attribution: Thomassé (also attributed in the Caccetta-Häggkvist circle)

entries.decoded: For every nonempty oriented graph D (0 < #|D|, no loops, no digons) and every δ with every vertex of out-degree at least δ, EITHER the longest directed simple path has length at least 2δ (ℓ(D) ≥ 2δ — the full conjecture conclusion), OR there exists a nonempty vertex set S (S ≠ ∅) of size at most δ (#|S| ≤ δ) such that every vertex v in S has at least 2δ − ℓ(D) of its out-neighbours inside S (outdeg_in S v ≥ 2δ − ℓ(D), nat truncated subtraction). The general structural lemma whose two branches drive the δ = 2 and δ = 3 results.

formal.name: lemma7
formal.verbatim:
Theorem lemma7 (D : orientedDigraph) (delta : nat) :
  0 < #|D| -> (forall v : D, delta <= outdeg v) ->
  2 * delta <= ell D \/
  exists S : {set D},
    [/\ S != set0, #|S| <= delta
      & forall v, v \in S -> 2 * delta - ell D <= outdeg_in S v].

entries.title: Linial–Berge path-partition duality

informal.text: Conjecture The minimum $ k $ -norm of a path partition on a directed graph $ D $ is no more than the maximal size of an induced $ k $ -colorable subgraph.

entries.decoded: For every digraph D and every k there exist a path-partition Q of V(D) (Q partitions every vertex into vertex-disjoint directed paths, pp_partition Q) and a vertex set S inducing a subdigraph with directed chromatic number ≤ k (dicolorableb (D[S]) k, i.e. χ⃗(D[S]) ≤ k) such that the k-norm of Q — the sum over paths of min(k, number of vertices on the path) — equals |S| (pp_knorm k Q = #|S|), and this is optimal: no path-partition Q' has k-norm smaller than |S| (forall Q' path-partition, #|S| ≤ pp_knorm k Q'). In other words the minimum k-norm over path-partitions equals the maximum order of an induced χ⃗ ≤ k subdigraph.

formal.name: linial_berge_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition linial_berge_statement : Prop :=
  forall (D : diGraphType) (k : nat),
    exists (Q : seq (D * seq D)) (S : {set D}),
      [/\ (* Q is a path-partition realizing the minimum k-norm *)
          pp_partition Q,
          (* S induces a χ⃗ ≤ k subdigraph (a union of k acyclic sets) *)
          dicolorableb (induced_digraph S) k,
          (* duality: the realized k-norm equals |S| *)
          pp_knorm k Q = #|S| &
          (* and this is optimal: no path-partition has smaller k-norm than |S|,
             i.e.

entries.title: Long directed cycles in diregular oriented graphs (Bermond–Germa–Heydemann–Sotteau)

informal.text: Conjecture Every strong oriented graph in which each vertex has indegree and outdegree at least $ d $ contains a directed cycle of length at least $ 2d+1 $ .

entries.decoded: For every nonempty oriented (digon-free) digraph D (0 < #|D|) and every d ≥ 1 such that D is strongly connected and every vertex has in-degree at least d and out-degree at least d, there is a directed cycle of length at least 2d+1.

formal.name: long_dicycle_diregular_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition long_dicycle_diregular_statement : Prop :=
  forall (D : orientedDigraph) (d : nat),
    (0 < #|D|)%N -> (0 < d)%N -> strongb D ->
    (forall v : D, (d <= indeg v)%N) -> (forall v : D, (d <= outdeg v)%N) ->
    exists c : seq D, dicycle c /\ (2 * d + 1 <= size c)%N.

entries.title: The m(3) landmark — a triangle-free oriented graph with dichromatic number ≥ 3

informal.text: The m(3) landmark: there exists an oriented triangle-free graph that is NOT 2-dicolourable (its dichromatic number is at least 3) — so the least order m(3) realizing dichromatic number 3 among oriented triangle-free graphs is finite.

source.name: arXiv 2403.02298
source.attribution: Aboulker, Bousquet, de Verclos et al.

entries.decoded: There exists a nonempty (0 < #|D|), digon-free digraph D whose underlying graph is triangle-free and that is NOT 2-dicolourable (its vertices cannot be split into 2 acyclic-inducing classes), so its dichromatic number is at least 3. This witnesses that the least order m(3) realizing dichromatic number 3 among oriented triangle-free graphs is finite.

formal.name: m3_landmark_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition m3_landmark_statement : Prop :=
  exists D : diGraphType,
    [/\ (0 < #|D|)%N, oriented_dg D, underlying_triangle_free D & ~~ dicolorableb D 2].

entries.title: Majority 3-colouring of Eulerian digraphs (KOSvZW, Open Problem 3)

informal.text: Open Problem 3 (1608.03040): does every Eulerian digraph have a majority 3-colouring?

source.name: arXiv 1608.03040
source.attribution: Kreutzer, Oum, Seymour, van der Zypen, Wood (2017)

entries.decoded: Every Eulerian digraph D (in-degree equals out-degree at every vertex) admits a majority 3-colouring: a 3-colouring of its vertices such that at every vertex v at most half of its out-neighbours share v's own colour (2·#{w : v→w, col w = col v} ≤ outdeg v for all v). The Eulerian special case of the majority 3-colouring conjecture.

formal.name: majority_3col_eulerian_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition majority_3col_eulerian_statement : Prop :=
  forall D : diGraphType, eulerian D ->
    exists col : D -> 'I_3, majority_col col.

entries.title: Majority 3-colouring Conjecture (Kreutzer–Oum–Seymour–van der Zypen–Wood, Conj 2)

informal.text: Conjecture 2 (1608.03040): every digraph admits a majority 3-colouring.

source.name: arXiv 1608.03040
source.attribution: Kreutzer, Oum, Seymour, van der Zypen, Wood (2017)

entries.decoded: Every digraph D admits a vertex colouring with 3 colours that is a majority colouring: at every vertex v at most half of its out-neighbours share v's own colour (encoded fraction-free as 2·#{w : v→w, col w = col v} ≤ outdeg v for all v).

formal.name: majority_3col_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition majority_3col_statement : Prop :=
  forall D : diGraphType, exists col : D -> 'I_3, majority_col col.

entries.title: Majority 3-colouring of tournaments (KOSvZW, Open Problem 2)

informal.text: Open Problem 2 (1608.03040): does every tournament have a majority 3-colouring?

source.name: arXiv 1608.03040
source.attribution: Kreutzer, Oum, Seymour, van der Zypen, Wood (2017)

entries.decoded: Every tournament T admits a majority 3-colouring: a colouring of its vertices with 3 colours such that at every vertex v at most half of its out-neighbours share v's own colour (2·#{w : v→w, col w = col v} ≤ outdeg v for all v). The tournament special case of the majority 3-colouring conjecture.

formal.name: majority_3col_tournament_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition majority_3col_tournament_statement : Prop :=
  forall T : tournament, exists col : T -> 'I_3, majority_col col.

entries.title: Fractional majority colouring (Kreutzer–Oum–Seymour–van der Zypen–Wood, Conj 9)

informal.text: Conjecture 9 (1608.03040): for every [k >= 2], every digraph admits a (k+1)-colouring with at most [(1/k)·deg⁺] same-coloured out-neighbours.

source.name: arXiv 1608.03040
source.attribution: Kreutzer, Oum, Seymour, van der Zypen, Wood (2017)

entries.decoded: For every k ≥ 2, every digraph D admits a vertex colouring with k+1 colours such that at every vertex v at most a (1/k)-fraction of its out-neighbours share v's colour (encoded fraction-free as k·#{w : v→w, col w = col v} ≤ outdeg v for all v). At k = 2 this is exactly the majority 3-colouring condition.

formal.name: majority_k1col_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition majority_k1col_statement : Prop :=
  forall (k : nat), 2 <= k ->
    forall D : diGraphType, exists col : D -> 'I_k.+1, kmajority_col k col.

entries.title: Matching-FAS = degreewidth at most 1 (sparse-tournament fact)

informal.text: The sparse-tournament fact (Davot–Isenmann–Roy–Thiebaut, arXiv:2212.06007): a tournament admits a matching feedback arc set iff its degreewidth is ≤ 1. Stated as a target Prop (it is known, but stated here uniformly, not proved).

entries.decoded: For every tournament T, T admits a matching feedback arc set if and only if its degreewidth Δ*(T) is at most 1. A matching-FAS is an arc set meeting every directed cycle whose underlying simple graph is a matching (a forest of maximum degree at most 1: no two of its arcs share a vertex). Δ*(T) is the minimum over all vertex orders of the maximum back-degree of a vertex (realized as a genuine minimum). Stated as a Prop target — a known result of Davot–Isenmann–Roy–Thiebaut stated uniformly here, not proved.

formal.name: matchingFAS_iff_dw1_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition matchingFAS_iff_dw1_statement : Prop :=
  forall T : tournament, has_matchingFAS T <-> (Delta_star T <= 1)%N.

entries.title: Infinitely many vertex-minimal LFO obstructions

informal.text: There is no largest vertex-minimal obstruction: for every [N] there is a vertex-minimal LFO-NO tournament on more than [N] vertices. (An infinite minimal-obstruction family would rule out a finite forbidden-subtournament characterization of Path-FAS; cf. the growing minimal-NO catalogues in path_matching_fas/docs/minimal_no_obstruction_catalogue.md.)

entries.decoded: There are arbitrarily large vertex-minimal LFO-obstructions: for every N there is a tournament T on more than N vertices (N < #|T|) that has no linear-forest ordering, yet is vertex-minimal — deleting ANY single vertex yields a tournament that DOES have a linear-forest ordering. So the family of vertex-minimal LFO-NO tournaments is infinite, which (via the conjectured Path-FAS ⇔ LFO reduction) would rule out any finite forbidden-subtournament characterization of Path-FAS.

formal.name: minimal_LFO_no_infinite_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition minimal_LFO_no_infinite_statement : Prop :=
  forall N : nat, exists T : tournament, minimal_LFO_no T /\ (N < #|T|)%N.

entries.title: Monochromatic reachability or rainbow triangles (Sands–Sauer–Woodrow variant)

informal.text: In an edge-colored digraph, we say that a subgraph is rainbow if all its edges have distinct colors, and monochromatic if all its edges have the same color. Problem Let $ G $ be a tournament with edges colored from a set of three colors. Is it true that $ G $ must have either a rainbow directed cycle of length three or a vertex $ v $ so that every other vertex can be reached from $ v $ by a monochromatic (directed) path?

source.name: OPG
source.attribution: Sands, Sauer, Woodrow (variant); posed by Bang-Jensen / Aharoni et al.

entries.decoded: For every nonempty tournament T (0 < #|T|) whose arcs are coloured with 3 colours, either T contains a rainbow directed triangle (a directed 3-cycle a→b→c→a whose three arcs carry three distinct colours) or T has a monochromatic-reachability root: a vertex v from which every vertex w is reachable by a single-colour directed path (the colour may depend on w).

formal.name: mono_reach_or_rainbow_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition mono_reach_or_rainbow_statement : Prop :=
  forall (T : tournament) (c : arc_colouring T 3),
    (0 < #|T|)%N ->
    rainbow_triangle c \/ exists v : T, mono_root c v.

entries.title: Cheng–Keevash δ = 3 endgame — no short strong 3-out-regular oriented digraph

informal.text: The endgame: no strong 3-outregular oriented digraph has ℓ ≤ 5

entries.decoded: It is NOT the case that a nonempty (0 < #|H|), strongly connected (strongb H), oriented digraph H (no loops, no digons) in which every vertex has out-degree EXACTLY 3 has its longest directed simple path of length at most 5: such an H with ℓ(H) ≤ 5 yields a contradiction (False). The internal endgame lemma forcing ℓ(H) ≥ 6 in the strong 3-out-regular case that underlies the δ = 3 theorem.

formal.name: no_short_strong3
formal.verbatim:
Lemma no_short_strong3 (H : orientedDigraph) :
  (forall v : H, outdeg v = 3) -> 0 < #|H| -> strongb H ->
  ell H <= 5 -> False.

entries.title: ω̄(ACₙ) = 3

informal.text: ω̄(ACₙ) = 3

entries.decoded: For m ≥ 3 (ACₙ a circulant tournament on n = 2m+1 ≥ 7 vertices), the directed clique number of ACₙ equals 3.

formal.name: omegabar_AC
formal.verbatim: Theorem omegabar_AC : (3 <= m)%N -> ω̄((AC m' : tournament)) = 3.

entries.title: ω̄(ACₙ − v) = 2
entries.decoded: For m ≥ 3 and any vertex v of ACₙ, deleting v from the circulant tournament ACₙ yields a tournament with directed clique number ω̄ = 2.

formal.name: omegabar_AC_del
formal.verbatim:
Theorem omegabar_AC_del (v : ACm) :
  (3 <= m)%N -> ω̄(del_tournament v) = 2.

entries.title: ω̄(T4 = ACₙ[C₃]) = 4
entries.decoded: The tournament T4 = ACₙ[C₃] (with n = 2m+1, m ≥ 3) has ω̄(T4) = 4, where ω̄ is the AACL clique-number invariant of a tournament. This is the value pinned for the 4-ω̄-criticality argument.

formal.name: omegabar_T4
formal.verbatim: Theorem omegabar_T4 : ω̄(T4) = 4.

entries.title: ω̄(T4 − v) = 3 for every vertex

informal.text: ω̄(T4 − v) = 3 for every vertex v

entries.decoded: For every vertex v of T4 = ACₙ[C₃] (n = 2m+1, m ≥ 3), the tournament obtained by deleting v has ω̄(T4 − v) = 3. (One value suffices for all v by vertex-transitivity of T4.) This is the criticality drop feeding T4_kcritical4.

formal.name: omegabar_T4_del
formal.verbatim: Theorem omegabar_T4_del (v : T4) : ω̄(del_tournament v) = 3.

entries.title: ω̄(T₅) = 5

informal.text: For every integer $k\geq 3$, there is an infinite number of $k$-$\operatorname{\overrightarrow{\omega}}$-critical tournaments.

source.name: arXiv 2310.04265
source.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (Clique number of tournaments, 2023)

entries.decoded: The directed clique number of the host tournament T₅ = ACₙ[ACₙ] (odd n = 2m+1, m ≥ 3) is exactly 5. This is the upper component of the criticality witness.

formal.name: omegabar_T5
formal.verbatim: Theorem omegabar_T5 : ω̄(T5) = 5.

entries.title: ω̄(T₅ − v) = 4 for every vertex v

informal.text: For every integer $k\geq 3$, there is an infinite number of $k$-$\operatorname{\overrightarrow{\omega}}$-critical tournaments.

source.name: arXiv 2310.04265
source.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (Clique number of tournaments, 2023)

entries.decoded: Deleting any single vertex v from the host tournament T₅ = ACₙ[ACₙ] yields a tournament with directed clique number exactly 4. By vertex-transitivity of the product, the value at one vertex spreads to all, so every single-vertex deletion drops ω̄ from 5 to 4.

formal.name: omegabar_T5_del
formal.verbatim: Theorem omegabar_T5_del (v : T5) : ω̄(del_tournament v) = 4.

entries.title: Vertex-deletion of ω̄ is vertex-independent on vertex-transitive tournaments

informal.text: The marquee theorem: on a vertex-transitive tournament, ω̄ after deletion is the same whichever vertex is deleted.

entries.decoded: If a tournament T is vertex-transitive (vertex_transitiveb T), then ω̄(del_tournament u) = ω̄(del_tournament v) for all vertices u, v — deleting any vertex leaves a subtournament with the same clique number, regardless of which vertex is removed.

formal.name: omegabar_del_vt
formal.verbatim:
Theorem omegabar_del_vt :
  vertex_transitiveb (T : diGraphType) ->
  forall u v : T, ω̄(del_tournament u) = ω̄(del_tournament v).

entries.title: ω̄ of a lexicographic substitution — lower bound

informal.text: The substitution lower bound: ω̄(S[H]) ≥ ω̄(S) + ω̄(H) − 1.

entries.decoded: For tournaments S and H, the lexicographic substitution S[H] (vertices are pairs (s,h), with (s₁,h₁) → (s₂,h₂) iff s₁ → s₂, or s₁ = s₂ and h₁ → h₂) satisfies ω̄(S) + ω̄(H) ≤ ω̄(S[H]) + 1, i.e. ω̄(S[H]) ≥ ω̄(S) + ω̄(H) − 1. The clique number is super-additive under substitution.

formal.name: omegabar_lexprod_ge
formal.verbatim:
Theorem omegabar_lexprod_ge :
  ω̄(S) + ω̄(H) <= ω̄(lexprod_tournament S H) + 1.

entries.title: ω̄(T) = 1 if and only if T is transitive
entries.decoded: For every nonempty tournament T (0 < #|T|), ω̄(T) equals 1 if and only if T is transitive (transb T, i.e. the arc relation is transitive). Equivalently, transitive tournaments are exactly the ones with clique number 1.

formal.name: omegabar_transb
formal.verbatim: Lemma omegabar_transb : 0 < #|T| -> (ω̄(T) == 1) = transb T.

entries.title: Oriented chromatic number of planar graphs is bounded (Courcelle; Sopena)

informal.text: An oriented colouring of an oriented graph is assignment $ c $ of colours to the vertices such that no two arcs receive ordered pairs of colours $ (c_1,c_2) $ and $ (c_2,c_1) $ . It is equivalent to a homomorphism of the digraph onto some tournament of order $ k $ . Problem What is the maximal possible oriented chromatic number of an oriented planar graph?

entries.decoded: There is a single number k such that every loopless digraph D whose underlying simple graph is planar (in the Wagner/minor sense: no K₅ and no K₃,₃ minor) admits an oriented k-colouring, encoded as a digraph homomorphism (arc-preserving map, not required surjective) from D to some tournament on exactly k vertices. The bound k is uniform over the whole class. Note: the formal statement quantifies over every loopless digraph with planar underlying graph (not only orientations); there is no 0 < #|D| nonemptiness guard.

formal.name: oriented_chromatic_planar_bounded_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition oriented_chromatic_planar_bounded_statement : Prop :=
  exists k : nat,
    forall (D : diGraphType) (llD : loopless D),
      planar_sg (underlyingG llD) -> oriented_kcolouring D k.

entries.title: Path-FAS = linear-forest ordering (the LFO reduction)

informal.text: The reduction (path_matching_fas/docs/path_fas.md): a tournament has a path-shaped feedback arc set iff some vertex order has a linear-forest back-arc graph. Stated as the formalization target (its proof is the open reduction).

entries.decoded: For every tournament T, T has a path-shaped feedback arc set if and only if T has a linear-forest ordering. The left side (has_pathFAS) asks for a set F of genuine arcs that meets every directed cycle and whose underlying simple graph (vertices adjacent iff F has an arc between them in either direction) is a linear forest — acyclic with every vertex of degree at most 2. The right side (has_LFO) asks for a vertex order whose back-arc graph is such a linear forest. Stated as a Prop target; its proof is the open reduction.

formal.name: pathFAS_iff_LFO_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition pathFAS_iff_LFO_statement : Prop :=
  forall T : tournament, has_pathFAS T <-> has_LFO T.

entries.title: Problem 6 (Aboulker–Bang-Jensen et al.) — bounded-mad ⇒ polynomial unavoidability bound

informal.text: Let $\alpha$ be a positive real number. Does there exist a polynomial $P_{\alpha}$ such that $\operatorname{unvd}(D)\leqslant P_{\alpha}(|V(D)|)$ for every digraph with maximum average degree at most $\alpha$?

source.name: arXiv 2410.23566
source.attribution: Aboulker, Bang-Jensen, Bousquet, Charbit, Havet et al.

entries.decoded: For every rational bound α, there exist nat coefficients a, d, b such that every nonempty digraph D (0 < #|D|) whose maximum average degree is at most α has unavoidability number nD bounded by the polynomial a·#|D|^d + b (here nD is pinned via the unvd relation: the least N such that every N-vertex tournament contains D). Faithfully encodes 'there exists a polynomial P_α with unvd(D) ≤ P_α(|V(D)|)' for bounded-mad digraphs, with the polynomial given concretely by coefficients a, d, b.

formal.name: prob6_unvd_statement
formal.verbatim: Definition prob6_unvd_statement : Prop := unvd.prob_6.

entries.title: AACL Question 5.9 — refuted at k = 3

informal.text: Is there a function $\ell$ such that, for every tournament $T$, if $\operatorname{\overrightarrow{\omega}}(T)\geq k$, then $T$ has a subtournament $A$ such that $|A|\leq\ell(k)$ and $\operatorname{\overrightarrow{\omega}}(A)\geq k$.

entries.decoded: For every bound L there exists a 3-ω̄-critical tournament T with more than L vertices such that the only subtournament S with ω̄(sub_tournament S) ≥ 3 is the whole vertex set T itself. So T witnesses ω̄ ≥ 3 only via an arbitrarily large subtournament, refuting the k = 3 case of Question 5.9 (which asked for a bounded-size, ℓ(k)-bounded, subtournament certifying ω̄ ≥ k).

formal.name: question_5_9_fails_at_k3
formal.verbatim:
Theorem question_5_9_fails_at_k3 (L : nat) :
  exists T : tournament,
    [/\ kcritical 3 T, (L < #|T|)%N
      & forall S : {set T}, (3 <= ω̄(sub_tournament S))%N -> S = [set: T]].

entries.title: AACL Question 5.9 fails at k = 4

informal.text: Is there a function $\ell$ such that, for every tournament $T$, if $\operatorname{\overrightarrow{\omega}}(T)\geq k$, then $T$ has a subtournament $A$ such that $|A|\leq\ell(k)$ and $\operatorname{\overrightarrow{\omega}}(A)\geq k$.

entries.decoded: For every bound L there is a tournament T that is 4-ω̄-critical, has more than L vertices, and in which the ONLY subtournament S with ω̄(S) ≥ 4 is the whole tournament (S = the full vertex set). So no subtournament strictly smaller than T attains ω̄ ≥ 4; since T can be made arbitrarily large, there is no bounded-size witness ℓ(4), refuting Question 5.9 at k = 4.

formal.name: question_5_9_fails_at_k4
formal.verbatim:
Theorem question_5_9_fails_at_k4 (L : nat) :
  exists T : tournament,
    [/\ kcritical 4 T, (L < #|T|)%N
      & forall S : {set T}, (4 <= ω̄(sub_tournament S))%N -> S = [set: T]].

entries.title: AACL Question 5.9 fails at k = 5

informal.text: Is there a function $\ell$ such that, for every tournament $T$, if $\operatorname{\overrightarrow{\omega}}(T)\geq k$, then $T$ has a subtournament $A$ such that $|A|\leq\ell(k)$ and $\operatorname{\overrightarrow{\omega}}(A)\geq k$.

source.name: arXiv 2310.04265
source.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (Clique number of tournaments, 2023)

entries.decoded: For every L there exists a tournament T that is 5-ω̄-critical, has more than L vertices, and whose ONLY subtournament S with ω̄ ≥ 5 is the whole tournament (S = the full vertex set). So there is no bound ℓ(5): one cannot witness ω̄ ≥ 5 inside a subtournament of bounded size, which is exactly what Question 5.9 asks for at k = 5 — hence Question 5.9 (the existence of such a bounding function ℓ) is NOT the case at k = 5.

formal.name: question_5_9_fails_at_k5
formal.verbatim:
Theorem question_5_9_fails_at_k5 (L : nat) :
  exists T : tournament,
    [/\ kcritical 5 T, (L < #|T|)%N
      & forall S : {set T}, (5 <= ω̄(sub_tournament S))%N -> S = [set: T]].

entries.title: AACL Question 5.9 — compact ω̄-cluster (f = id form)

informal.text: Is there a function $\ell$ such that, for every tournament $T$, if $\operatorname{\overrightarrow{\omega}}(T)\geq k$, then $T$ has a subtournament $A$ such that $|A|\leq\ell(k)$ and $\operatorname{\overrightarrow{\omega}}(A)\geq k$.

entries.decoded: There exists a single function ℓ : ℕ → ℕ such that for every tournament T and every k, if ω̄(T) ≥ k then T has a vertex subset S of size at most ℓ(k) whose induced subtournament already satisfies ω̄(sub_tournament S) ≥ k. This is the f = id (threshold exactly k) strengthening of Conjecture 5.8; Conjecture 5.10 refutes it.

formal.name: question_5_9_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition question_5_9_statement : Prop :=
  exists ell : nat -> nat,
    forall (T : tournament) (k : nat), (k <= ω̄(T))%N ->
      exists S : {set T}, (#|S| <= ell k)%N /\ (k <= ω̄(sub_tournament S))%N.

entries.title: Sands–Sauer–Woodrow monochromatic reachability (arc-coloured digraphs)

informal.text: Conjecture For every $ k $ , there exists an integer $ f(k) $ such that if $ D $ is a digraph whose arcs are colored with $ k $ colors, then $ D $ has a $ S $ set which is the union of $ f(k) $ stables sets so that every vertex has a monochromatic path to some vertex in $ S $ .

entries.decoded: For every number of colours k there is a bound f such that every k-arc-coloured digraph D has a vertex set S which is a union of at most f stable sets and such that every vertex v of D reaches some vertex of S by a monochromatic directed path (a path whose arcs all carry one colour; the colour and target may depend on v).

formal.name: sands_sauer_woodrow_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition sands_sauer_woodrow_statement : Prop :=
  forall k : nat, exists f : nat,
    forall (D : diGraphType) (c : arc_colouring D k),
      exists S : {set D},
        [/\ union_of_stables f S
          & forall v : D, exists2 s : D, s \in S & mono_reach_any c v s].

entries.title: Seymour's Second Neighbourhood Conjecture

informal.text: Conjecture Any oriented graph has a vertex whose outdegree is at most its second outdegree.

entries.decoded: Every nonempty oriented (digon-free) digraph D (0 < #|D|) has a "Seymour vertex": there exists a vertex v whose first out-neighbourhood is no larger than its second out-neighbourhood, i.e. outdeg(v) ≤ #|Nout2(v)|, where Nout2(v) is the set of vertices reachable from v by a directed path of length exactly 2 (and not already out-neighbours of v).

formal.name: seymour_second_neighbourhood_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition seymour_second_neighbourhood_statement : Prop :=
  forall D : orientedDigraph,
    (0 < #|D|)%N -> exists v : D, (outdeg v <= #|Nout2 v|)%N.

entries.title: Splitting a digraph with minimum out-degree constraints (Alon; Thomassen)

informal.text: Problem Is there a minimum integer $ f(d) $ such that the vertices of any digraph with minimum outdegree $ d $ can be partitioned into two classes so that the minimum outdegree of the subgraph induced by each class is at least $ d $ ?

entries.decoded: There is a function f : nat → nat such that for every digraph D and every d, if every vertex has out-degree at least f(d), then the vertex set splits into a proper bipartition (V1 nonempty and not all of D, with complement ~:V1) in which each part induces minimum out-degree at least d — every v in V1 has outdeg_in(V1, v) ≥ d, and every v outside V1 has outdeg_in(~:V1, v) ≥ d, where outdeg_in(A,v) counts only arcs whose head lies in A.

formal.name: splitting_min_outdegree_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition splitting_min_outdegree_statement : Prop :=
  exists f : nat -> nat,
    forall (D : diGraphType) (d : nat),
      (forall v : D, (f d <= outdeg v)%N) ->
      exists V1 : {set D},
        [/\ (* a PROPER bipartition: both parts nonempty (without this, V1 := setT makes
               the complement-side constraint vacuous, trivializing the statement) *)
            V1 != set0,
            V1 != [set: D],
            (forall v : D, v \in V1 -> (d <= outdeg_in V1 v)%N)
          & (forall v : D, v \notin V1 -> (d <= outdeg_in (~: V1) v)%N)].

entries.title: Stable set meeting all longest directed paths (Laborde–Payan–Xuong)

informal.text: Conjecture Every digraph has a stable set meeting all longest directed paths

entries.decoded: Every digraph D (no nonemptiness guard) has a stable set S (an independent set: no two of its vertices are joined by an arc in either direction) that meets every longest directed path — for every directed path x :: s whose LENGTH (number of arcs, size s) attains the maximum ell(D) = max over dipaths of size s (so the path has ell(D) arcs and ell(D)+1 vertices, NOT ell(D) vertices), some vertex of S lies on that path (v ∈ x :: s).

formal.name: stable_meeting_longest_dipaths_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition stable_meeting_longest_dipaths_statement : Prop :=
  forall D : diGraphType,
    exists S : {set D}, stable S /\
      forall (x : D) (s : seq D), dipath x s -> size s = ell D ->
        exists2 v : D, v \in S & v \in x :: s.

entries.title: Maximum dichromatic number of oriented triangle-free graphs — combinatorial core (t⃗)

informal.text: For any oriented star $S$, $\mathrm{Forb}(S)$ is $\chi$-bounded.

source.name: arXiv 2403.02298
source.attribution: Aboulker, Bousquet, de Verclos et al.

entries.decoded: There is a sequence h : nat -> nat such that, for every order n > 0: (upper bound) every digon-free digraph D on exactly n vertices with triangle-free underlying graph is h(n)-dicolourable (χ⃗(D) ≤ h(n)); and (attainment) some such order-n graph is NOT (h(n)−1)-dicolourable, pinning h(n) to the exact maximum dichromatic number t⃗(n). This is the combinatorial core of the Θ(√(n/log n)) conjecture, with the asymptotic envelope abstracted into the existence of such a binding sequence h.

formal.name: tvec_core_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition tvec_core_statement : Prop :=
  exists h : nat -> nat,
    forall n : nat, (0 < n)%N ->
      (* upper bound: every order-n oriented triangle-free D is h n-dicolourable *)
      (forall D : diGraphType,
         #|D| = n -> oriented_dg D -> underlying_triangle_free D ->
         dicolorableb D (h n)) /\
      (* attained — pins h to the MAXIMUM t⃗(n): some order-n such D is not
         (h n − 1)-dicolourable (without the upper bound, h := 1 makes (h n).-1 = 0 and
         the clause holds vacuously).

entries.title: k-ω̄-criticality of a vertex-transitive tournament reduces to one deletion

informal.text: Hence k-ω̄-criticality reduces to a single deletion.

entries.decoded: For a vertex-transitive tournament T (vertex_transitiveb T) and any fixed vertex v₀, T is k-ω̄-critical if and only if ω̄(T) = k and ω̄(del_tournament v₀) = k−1. Vertex-transitivity makes all single-vertex deletions interchangeable, so the universally-quantified deletion test in the definition of kcritical collapses to a single arbitrary deletion at v₀.

formal.name: vt_kcritical
formal.verbatim:
Theorem vt_kcritical (k : nat) (v0 : T) :
  vertex_transitiveb (T : diGraphType) ->
  kcritical k T = (ω̄(T) == k) && (ω̄(del_tournament v0) == k.-1).

entries.title: Woodall's Conjecture (directed Lucchesi–Younger)

informal.text: Conjecture If $ G $ is a directed graph with smallest directed cut of size $ k $ , then $ G $ has $ k $ disjoint dijoins.

entries.decoded: For every digraph D and every k, if the minimum dicut size is k — encoded as: some one-way set B (nonempty proper set with no arc entering it) has dicut size exactly k, and every one-way set has dicut size at least k — then there exist k dijoins (arc selectors each meeting the dicut of every one-way set) that are pairwise arc-disjoint (all dijoin Js, size Js = k, and for all i ≠ j the i-th and j-th selectors share no arc). I.e. the minimum dicut size equals the maximum number of arc-disjoint dijoins.

formal.name: woodall_statement
formal.verbatim:
Definition woodall_statement : Prop :=
  forall (D : diGraphType) (k : nat),
    (exists B : {set D}, oneway B /\ dicut_size B = k) ->
    (forall B : {set D}, oneway B -> (k <= dicut_size B)%N) ->
    exists Js : seq (D -> {set D}),
      [/\ all dijoin Js,
          size Js = k &
          (* pairwise arc-disjoint *)
          [forall i : 'I_(size Js), forall j : 'I_(size Js),
             (i != j) ==>
             arc_disjoint_sel (nth (fun _ => set0) Js i) (nth (fun _ => set0) Js j)]].
